creator: rhurlin@FreeBSD.org
text:
2.0.8 builds and installs fine on my 9.9-CURRENT and 10.0-CURRENT 
(amd64) boxes. So I have no clue what the exact problem could be.

I suppose there is some trouble with the patch 2.0.7 to 2.0.8? After 
patching there remain *.orig files and a 'files' directory which should 
have been removed before building 2.0.8, am I right?

#ls -l
total 28
-rw-r--r--  1 root  wheel  3078 29 Nov 07:33 Makefile
-rw-r--r--  1 root  wheel  2955 20 Okt 09:52 Makefile.orig
-rw-r--r--  1 root  wheel   129 29 Nov 07:33 distinfo
-rw-r--r--  1 root  wheel   129 22 Mai  2011 distinfo.orig
drwxr-xr-x  2 root  wheel   512 29 Nov 07:33 files
-rw-r--r--  1 root  wheel   500  4 Sep  2010 pkg-descr
-rw-r--r--  1 root  wheel  1130 17 Nov  2009 pkg-message
-rw-r--r--  1 root  wheel  2538 29 Nov 07:33 pkg-plist
-rw-r--r--  1 root  wheel  4295 17 Jul 07:45 pkg-plist.orig


I am also aware of a problem with powerpc, which does exist for some 
time now:

http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/powerpc-errorlogs/e.9.20111111050840/saga-2.0.7_4.log

Unfortunately I have no powerpc box. Perhaps it is a workaround to 
exclude powerpc arch until we found a solution?

.if ${ARCH} == "powerpc"
BROKEN=fails to build on powerpc: conflicting declaration types
.endif


Could you please give me a hint what is meant with 'build failed'?
